node.title: Uber | OA | find subarrays with at least k unique numbers

topic.title: Uber | OA | find subarrays with at least k unique numbers

post.content:
Giving an array and an int k;
find how mang subarrays which has at least k unique numbers(unique number means this number only appear once in this subarray)
for example:
[1, 2,1,1] k = 2
output : 2 
[1, 2] and [2, 1]
[1, 2, 1] is unvalid because only have one unique number;

At first I tried two pointers left and right, when unique < right, keep right++; when unique == k then res += (len - right)
but soon I realized that is wrong because for example
[1, 2, 3, 4,1,2,5] k = 3
for left = 0, first I can find right = 2, but [1, 2, 3, 4] and [1, 2, 3,4,1] is valid and [1, 2, 3, 4,1, 2] is not, then [1, 2, 3,4,1,2,5] is valid again.
At last I gave a bruce force solution but couldn't able to solve the hidden cases.
Anyone please give an optimal solution?
did the interviewer give you the optimal answer?

Hi there!
If you could please edit the post title to the following layout: Company Name | Stage (Phone, Onsite, OA) | Question name
We ask that you can follow these guidelines [here](https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-question/126698/How-to-write-an-Interview-Question-post), thank you!

First of all the number of sub Arrays that will be created will always = ```arr.length/k``` (if it if given that the testcases will always generate subArrays)
and for the sub Arrays:
the maximum length of a sub Array will be ```k + (arr.length%k)```
We declare an variable which will keep track of the number of elements that are not making a sub Array  and that will be ```notDecided = (arr.length%k)```
So, we iterate through the array to fill a sub Array until we reach an element which is already present in the sub Array or the length of the sub Array becomes ```k + (arr.length%k)``` . 
Now we update the ```notDecided``` variable:
```
if(subArray.length > k) {
     notDecided -= (subArray.length-k);
}
```
And we keep doing this until we have bagged all the elements in any sub Array.
I hope this helped. I am terrible in explaining things.
(if you think my solution is wrong, please do explain it)
Sliding Window probably, if the number of unique elements in the subarray is < k then increase the window otherwise as soon as unique elements == k start reducing the window
